title: Silene ismailitica
intro: Silene ismailitica, also known as Israeli campion, is a flowering plant species native to the Middle East, characterized by its distinctive white or pink flowers and hairy leaves. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation, pain, and respiratory problems, due to its reported health benefits. The bioactive constituents of Silene ismailitica include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. Herbal preparations made from Silene ismailitica include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used in traditional medicine to relieve symptoms of colds, fever, and digestive issues.

This family comprises around 100 genera, with Caryophyllaceae being one of the largest and most diverse families of flowering plants.
